idea-plugin.name: DependencyUpdater
idea-plugin.description:
The ultimate dependency manager and security scanner for IntelliJ IDEA

DependencyUpdater is a powerful plugin designed explicitly for Maven and Gradle projects. It acts as your intelligent assistant to keep your build files clean, updated, and secure.

Key Features:

  • 🚀 Full Maven & Gradle Support: Seamlessly parses pom.xml, build.gradle / build.gradle.kts and libs.versions.toml files.
  • 🧩 Gradle Plugins Update: Support for scanning and updating Gradle plugins configured within plugins {} block (both Groovy and Kotlin DSLs).
  • 🛡️ Security Scanning (OSV API): Automatically detects Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE) in your direct and transitive dependencies.
  • 🔄 Smart Version Updates: Discovers newer library versions directly from your configured artifact repositories. Automatically filters out already up-to-date dependencies for clean, distraction-free lists.
  • 🏢 Enterprise Ready: Built-in native support for Corporate Proxies and custom SSL Certificates (JFrog Artifactory compatibility).
  • 🔐 Custom Repositories: Connects to private registries with safe Bearer Token and Basic authentication. Includes predefined HTML directory listing scanner for private JFrog instances.
  • 🎯 LTS & Beta Filtering: Advanced version recognition differentiating between Long Term Support (LTS) releases and experimental Beta/Snapshot builds.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
Version 1.5
  • Smart Up-to-Date Filtering: The Direct Dependencies list now intelligently hides libraries that are already at their latest versions, keeping your view focused and distraction-free. Switching between LTS and Beta modes dynamically updates visibility based on available builds.
  • Advanced LTS/Beta Recognition: Enhanced version parsing engine that accurately classifies corporate naming conventions and JFrog Artifactory builds (such as timestamped snapshots, -dev, -preview, and -ea qualifiers).
  • Streamlined Transitive Scanning: Transitive dependency analysis has been streamlined to focus purely on background vulnerability detection (CVE scanning) and version auditing, removing automatic build file modification for safer builds.

Version 1.4
  • Gradle Plugins Support: Full parsing and auto-updating functionality for Gradle plugins {} blocks (Groovy, Kotlin DSL) and TOML plugin references.
  • Corporate Proxy & SSL: Migrated underlying HTTP engine to IntelliJ native HttpRequests for robust handling of Enterprise Proxies and Custom SSL Certificates.
  • JFrog Artifactory (HTML): Dedicated predefined repository type for private JFrog instances utilizing HTML directory listings. Automatically scans both libs-release and libs-snapshot concurrently.
  • Improved History View: Reintroduced visibility of older versions in the dropdown list to accurately reflect past security vulnerabilities (CVEs).
  • Bugfixes: Patched minor UI glitches, introduced dynamic authentication labels, and optimized regex matchers for libs.versions.toml syntax.

Version 1.3
  • Custom Repositories: Added a new Settings page to manage multiple custom artifact repositories.
  • Global Predefined Defaults: Quickly add standard global repositories (Maven Central, Google, Spring, JBoss) from a dedicated dropdown menu.
  • Secure Authentication: Full support for Basic (Username/Password) and Bearer Token authentication via IntelliJ PasswordSafe. UI dynamically adapts to your Auth Type.
  • Concurrent Fetching: The plugin now queries all configured repositories simultaneously to fetch the latest dependency versions.
  • Standalone Compatibility: Removed hard IDE constraints. The plugin now works flawlessly even on strictly restricted corporate IDE instances where official Maven or Gradle plugins are disabled.

Version 1.1
  • IDE Compatibility: Updated to support IntelliJ IDEA 2026.1 and fixed deprecated FilenameIndex API usages.
  • Marketplace Compliance: Updated plugin ID and configured proper untilBuild limits.
  • Code Quality: Refactored codebase, removed unused comments, and bumped Gradle Wrapper to 9.5.1 with updated Mockito framework.

Version 1.0
  • Initial Release: Scans Maven and Gradle projects for outdated dependencies.
  • Security Scanning: Integrates with OSV API to expose Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE) for direct and transitive dependencies.
  • LTS Filtering: Highlights Long Term Support versions for safer upgrades.
